name: OpenVPN
description: OpenVPN Access Server — run your own VPN with a built-in admin web UI and client config downloads. The free tier allows 2 simultaneous connections.
compose:
name: "slashnode-openvpn"
services:
  openvpn:
    image: "openvpn/openvpn-as:2.14.3-5936bcd7-Ubuntu24"
    container_name: "openvpn"
    restart: "unless-stopped"
    networks:
      - slashnode
    cap_add:
      - NET_ADMIN
    devices:
      - "/dev/net/tun"
    ports:
      - "1194:1194/udp"
      - "127.0.0.1:943:943"
    volumes:
      - "openvpn-data:/openvpn"
networks:
  slashnode:
    external: true
volumes:
  openvpn-data:
    name: "slashnode-openvpn_openvpn_openvpn-data"
notes: OpenVPN Access Server. The VPN listens on 1194/udp (public, so clients can connect). The admin UI is HTTPS on 943, published only on 127.0.0.1 — reach it via the app's .onion (https://<onion>:943/admin) or an SSH tunnel. On first start set the admin password: open the openvpn console and run  cd /usr/local/openvpn_as/scripts && ./sacli --user openvpn --new_pass='YOUR-STRONG-PASSWORD' SetLocalPassword && ./sacli start  — then log in to /admin as user 'openvpn' to create users and download client profiles.
